Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

build failing in el7 due to lack of defn for u_int in userspace.  think changing them all to 'unsigned int' would be less ambiguous, more portable, and could be the right fix.

Patch Set 1:

u_int is a BSD thing which you can find in sys/types.h. Can be very nasty especially if you use gcc -std=c99. It will not build in that case.  I agree about the use of unsigned int or if you really need strict types use uint32_t and friends which are apart of the C99 standard.

Patch Set 1:

Given your advices, I choose to replace u_int with unsigned int.

Patch Set 4:

I tried building this on my rhel6.6 build box and it fails:
 gss_oids.c:32:27: error: gssapi/gssapi.h: No such file or directory
 gss_oids.c:35: error: expected ???=???, ???,???, ???;???, ???asm??? or ???__attribute__??? before ???krb5oid???
 gss_oids.c:44: error: expected ???=???, ???,???, ???;???, ???asm??? or ???__attribute__??? before ???nulloid???
 gss_oids.c:48: error: expected ???=???, ???,???, ???;???, ???asm??? or ???__attribute__??? before ???skoid???
 gss_oids.c:53: error: expected ???=???, ???,???, ???;???, ???asm??? or ???__attribute__??? before ???spkm3oid???
 make[4]: *** [lsvcgssd-gss_oids.o] Error 1
 make[4]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....

Patch Set 4:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

we need to get to the root of build failure I am seeing as I cannot land it otherwise.

Patch Set 4:

Oleg. the #include you are missing is in the libgssglue-devel rpm.  I bet if you install that your build will be fine.

More evidence the autoconf changes are no longer forbidding build of gss when needed #includes aren't there.

Patch Set 4:

An #include with a similar name is also in the krb5-devel rpm.

Patch Set 4:

I thought libgssglue and libgssapi no longer exist on RHEL7? If that is the case then Bob's solution to use the headers from the krb5-devel rpms sounds right.

Patch Set 4:

James, you are correct there is no libgssglue in el7, but Oleg was building on el6.6.  I think one of the things this mod does is eliminate the need for libgssglue, so probably install of krb5-devel is what is needed.

I have both on all my el6.6 nodes.

Patch Set 4:

should we be including necessary configure/autoconf changes here to detect missing includes and disabling functionality needed there?
That's kind of what configure is for?

Patch Set 4:

my opinion is as long as it does build ok with everything needed in the build environment we may decide it's ok as is.  I agree it would be better if autoconf correctly detected the lack of needed #includes and disabled building gss.  I think without this mod it did so in the past.

Patch Set 4:

In both rhel6 and rhel7, /usr/include/gssapi/gssapi.h is brought by the same rpm as /usr/include/gssapi/gssapi_krb5.h, which is krb5-devel rpm.
Given that gssapi/gssapi_krb5.h is tested in AC_KERBEROS_V5 check (lustre/autoconf/kerberos5.m4), how can we end up building with GSS support when gssapi/gssapi.h is missing? In my build environment, the absence of gssapi/gssapi_krb5.h disables GSS support.

I agree autoconf/configure is not robust to the situation where gssapi/gssapi_krb5.h is there but gssapi/gssapi.h is not, but we can consider this is a corner case, and possibly address it in another patch not dedicated to 3.1x kernel support.

Patch Set 4:

On my Ubuntu15 system the gssapi headers points to /usr/include/mit-krb5/gssapi/*. Oleg in your setup do you have this path?

parents.subject: LU-6356 ptlrpc: add a 'enc_pool_max_memory_mb' module param

commit.subject: LU-6490 gss: 3.1x kernels adjustments for gssapi code
commit.message:
LU-6490 gss: 3.1x kernels adjustments for gssapi code

There are a number of changes in 3.1x kernels concerning the GSSAPI:
- libgssapi and libgssglue do not exist anymore, so call krb5
  primitives directly, and remove associated config checks;
- struct cred has no tgcred member anymore, so use cred directly;
- struct key_type instantiate and update function prototypes
  have changed;
- add new config checks on struct cred and struct key_type;
- u_int is BSD specific, so it is replaced with unsigned int.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Change-Id: I3b13c2afcb4b800bdcffb3b8713048f8e39f6866
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15342
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
